human_verification.notes: Verified ambulatory-specific records, source classes, access and amendment boundaries, adjacent-lane routing, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit refusal, escalation, and ambulatory-specific evidence behavior.
Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack names ambulatory-specific artifacts including encounter notes, addenda, orders, referrals, result attachments, ROI records, amendment trails, and signature evidence.
Boundary handling is explicit for clinical authorship, coding or billing determination, privacy or release authority, legal sufficiency, and official-record approval decisions.
Retention, access, amendment, and source-conflict questions are escalated instead of overresolved.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; state-law, portal-release, and source-precedence variance is converted into required retrieval or escalation behavior.
Package is ready for validated spec-pack use and future retained-implementation promotion if repeated runtime demand justifies it.
